items.title: Russia’s Mikron is selling framed test wafers with up to 120,000 processors as souvenirs — 12 designs, priced around $170 each, sold alongside $2 vials of cleanroom air
items.description: Russia’s Mikron is earning a bit of cash on the side by selling picture-framed test wafers as souvenirs with 12 designs to choose from, priced around $170.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Russia’s Mikron is earning a bit of cash on the side by selling picture-framed test wafers as souvenirs. There are 12 designs in the product gallery, all priced at 12,500 rubles (~$170), with various frame backgrounds and commemorative texts. Mikron says supplies of these exclusive silicon wafer artworks are limited. (Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingSpotted by Dmitrii Kuznetsov @torgeek on X, some of these wafers will feature the AMUR MIK32 RISC-V chip, produced in Russia since 2022. The computer and data science enthusiast also notes that Mikron is busy “preparing to release a new RISC-V — MIK32-2.” (machine translation)The souvenir product pages also reveal that the framed 200mm wafers may also come packing between 30 to 120,000 chips, depending on the product. For example, one of the wafer designs may be full of transport card chips as used by the Moscow Metro.Latest Videos From You may like 11-month old Russian outfit claims it has developed 16-core and 32-core chips, flaunts Cyrillic-badged chips 'Silicon' is a new five-pound art book charting the semiconductor revolution with full-page die shots and commentary Nexperia China claims to be making chips using 12-inch wafers Buyers visiting the Mikorn souvenir shop pages will choose their framed wafer by selecting the frame color and/or theme. For example, there are products with simple black or white frames. But there are other designs with lace, paisley, or even an outer space theme. What kind of wafer you get with your choice of frame, appears to be pot luck.We used machine translation on the new 2026 edition wafer souvenirs, to see what the information panel to the lower left of the frame said. The top row of the table reads: 2026 | Made in Russia | Techno exclusive | Russian chips | 200mm. Moving to the second row: Silicon wafer with integrated circuits | The satellite plate is used in the technological process for quality control at all stages of microcircuit production.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)(Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)(Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)If you manage to buy one of these Russian semiconductor industry souvenirs, you will need a space on your wall for a 270 x 270 x 15mm (10.6 x 10.6 x 0.6-inches) frame, which weighs 365g (~13 ounces). I think they look smart, but it would be even better if you had a piece of tech that actually used one of the chips from the wafers for your wall presentation.If you have followed the link to Mikron’s souvenir shop and had a browse around, like us, there are a few other interesting trinkets to see. A much cheaper souvenir flagged as ‘new’ is the small tube of air from the firm’s NWP clean room in Zelenograd, priced at around $2. Elsewhere there are gift shop trinkets of the style you might find anywhere, like dolls, mugs, magnets, and puzzles.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Mark Tyson
items.titleZh: 俄罗斯Mikron公司出售装裱测试晶圆作为纪念品——内含多达12万个处理器，12种设计，每片约170美元，同时销售2美元一瓶的洁净室空气
items.descriptionZh:
俄罗斯芯片制造商Mikron正通过销售嵌有测试晶圆的装饰画框来获取额外收入。这些晶圆纪念品共有12种设计，售价统一为12,500卢布（约合170美元），可根据客户需求选择不同的画框背景和纪念文字。Mikron表示，这些独家硅晶圆艺术品的供应量有限。

这些晶圆中的一部分搭载了AMUR MIK32 RISC-V芯片——该芯片自2022年起在俄罗斯量产。据计算机和数据科学爱好者Dmitrii Kuznetsov在X平台上爆料，Mikron正在“准备发布新款RISC-V芯片——MIK32-2”（机器翻译）。此外，纪念品页面显示，这些镶嵌在画框中的200毫米晶圆可能包含30至12万颗芯片，具体数量取决于产品类型。例如，其中一款晶圆设计上密集排列着莫斯科地铁使用的交通卡芯片。

从技术原理角度看，这些晶圆本质上是半导体制造过程中的测试晶圆（test wafers），通常用于质量控制和工艺验证。它们是在晶圆厂中经过完整光刻、刻蚀、沉积等步骤生产的，但最终并不封装成独立芯片，而是作为检查光刻对准精度、膜厚均匀性、缺陷密度等参数的标准片。Mikron将其作为纪念品出售，既展现了其制造工艺，又实现了废物利用。其中搭载的RISC-V芯片属于开源指令集架构，具有模块化、低功耗、可定制等优势，AMUR MIK32是俄罗斯首款基于RISC-V的微控制器，用于物联网和嵌入式场景。而MIK32-2可能是在性能、功耗或集成度上进一步优化的迭代版本。

在性能参数方面，晶圆直径为200毫米（约8英寸），属于成熟制程常用的规格（相比当前主流的300毫米晶圆，200毫米产线更适合小批量、多品种的模拟/混合信号芯片）。单张晶圆上芯片数量从30到12万颗不等，差异极大，说明这些晶圆可能来自不同设计或不同工艺节点（例如大尺寸的微控制器芯片数量少，而小尺寸的交通卡芯片数量多）。画框成品尺寸为270×270×15毫米（约10.6×10.6×0.6英寸），重量365克（约13盎司），适合挂墙展示。

除晶圆画框外，Mikron商店还出售更具噱头的纪念品：一管来自其Zelenograd洁净室的“空气”，售价约2美元，以及常规的玩偶、马克杯、磁贴、拼图等。值得注意的是，晶圆画框左下角的信息标签（据机器翻译）显示：“2026 | 俄罗斯制造 | 技术独家 | 俄罗斯芯片 | 200毫米”，第二行写着“带集成电路的硅晶圆 | 该卫星板用于微电路生产所有阶段的质量控制工艺”。这暗示这些晶圆可能来自2026年（或许是未来纪念版），并且强调了其作为质量监控“卫星片”（monitor wafer）的工业用途。

技术影响和应用场景方面：Mikron此举不仅是一种商业尝试，更具有技术品牌传播意义。通过将高价值的半导体制造过程转化为艺术藏品，它向公众展示了俄罗斯在芯片制造领域的能力——尽管受西方制裁限制，但依然具备200毫米晶圆量产能力（主要用于成熟制程，如90nm及以上节点）。对于收藏者和科技爱好者来说，这些晶圆提供了直观了解芯片微观结构的窗口。同时，这也反映了俄罗斯半导体产业在寻求多元化收入，以及通过RISC-V架构实现技术自主的战略方向。未来，若MIK32-2芯片成功量产，可能进一步推动俄罗斯物联网、智能卡、工业控制等领域摆脱对国外架构的依赖。而晶圆纪念品的销售，也能在一定程度上向全球展示其技术成果，甚至作为外交或文化输出的载体。

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 boots up in just 4 seconds instead of 90 with new Advanced Shader Delivery tech and AMD GPUs — Microsoft claims 95% reduction in gaming load times
items.description: Microsoft is bringing Advanced Shader Delivery to Windows 11 PCs at large, after introducing the tech on Xbox ROG Ally handhelds last year. The company says you can expect up to 95% faster load times in Forza Horizon 6, for instance, going from 90 seconds to just 4 seconds on initial launch thanks to precompiled shaders.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft announced Advanced Shader Delivery (ASD) for Xbox ROG Ally devices last year, bringing precompiled shaders to the handheld to improve load times. Since then, ASD has been included in the DirectX SDK, with both Intel and Nvidia already releasing their own versions of the tech. Today, AMD joins them as Microsoft expands ASD beyond handhelds to RDNA 3, RDNA 3.5, and RDNA 4 GPUs.Forza Horizon 6 is the latest game to feature ASD on Windows 11 PCs, but you need the Microsoft Store/Xbox PC app version to take advantage of it. Using an RX 7600 GPU and a Ryzen 7 5800 CPU, Advanced Shader Delivery helped the game boot up 95% faster, taking only four seconds to load on first launch. Without ASD enabled, Forza Horizon 6 took nearly a minute and a half to load otherwise.This is because every time you install a new game or go through an update, the game needs a fresh shader cache, which can take a few minutes to rebuild. Even if you don't touch the game, but your GPU drivers are updated, you need to recompile shaders for all of your games when you open them for the first time. This process is necessary because shader compilation is performed on-device and must account for hardware variability.Latest Videos From (Image credit: Microsoft)Microsoft's solution is to decouple shaders from drivers entirely and place them in a Precompiled Shader Database (PSDB) that lives in the cloud. Every time you download a game from the Microsoft Store or the Xbox PC app, ASD detects your specific configuration (game, GPU, driver) and downloads the precompiled shaders in advance. So, when you open the game, the shaders are already compiled, and you don't have to wait.Consoles have done this forever, which makes sense considering they don't have to worry about different hardware configs. Even Valve has a version of precompiled shaders for Linux that it developed for the Steam Deck, but never ported to Windows. Since SSDs have made long load times a thing of the past, this has been one of the last remaining hurdles to instantaneous game launches. "PC Gaming" preview in the Xbox Insider Hub (Image credit: Future)Advanced Shader Delivery is available only on RDNA 3 and later GPUs and only on games downloaded via the Xbox PC app or the Microsoft Store. We don't know when other Windows marketplaces will adopt it, but at least 34 other games should support ASD right away, since they were part of the original announcement for the Xbox ROG Ally handhelds. Moreover, you need to be enrolled in the Xbox Insiders program to get the Xbox Insiders Hub app, which is required to enable Advanced Shader Delivery, since Microsoft is classifying this as a preview. The company worked closely with AMD to develop it, which is why it's limited to RDNA 3+ hardware for now, because otherwise, it's supposed to be a universal solution for all GPUs on Windows 11 going forward.Regardless, if you have an Nvidia GPU, the Nvidia app should have a feature called "Auto Shader Compilation" that does the same thing. It gives you more granular control over the size of the shader cache, too. If you're rocking an Intel GPU, then check out "Precompiled Shader Distribution" in the Intel Graphics app instead. Intel has implied that it's using its own cloud database for now, with the ASD standard being adopted later this year. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 借助新高级着色器传输技术和AMD GPU，《极限竞速：地平线6》启动仅需4秒而非90秒——微软声称游戏加载时间减少95%
items.descriptionZh:
微软宣布将其高级着色器交付（Advanced Shader Delivery，ASD）技术扩展至AMD RDNA 3、RDNA 3.5和RDNA 4架构的GPU，结束了此前仅限Xbox ROG Ally掌机以及英特尔和NVIDIA单独实现该功能的局面。ASD的核心意义在于解决PC游戏启动时因着色器编译导致的漫长等待——每次安装新游戏、更新游戏或升级显卡驱动后，系统都需要在本地重新编译着色器缓存，这一过程可能耗时数分钟。微软的方案是将着色器与驱动彻底解耦，将其预编译后存储于云端“预编译着色器数据库”（PSDB）。当用户从微软商店或Xbox PC应用下载游戏时，ASD会自动识别用户的游戏、GPU和驱动配置，提前下载匹配的预编译着色器，从而在首次启动时无需等待编译，实现即开即玩。

在性能测试中，使用RX 7600显卡和Ryzen 7 5800 CPU的PC运行《极限竞速：地平线6》时，开启ASD后游戏首次启动仅需4秒，相比未开启时近90秒的加载时间，提速高达95%。这一提升直接消除了SSD普及后游戏启动的最后一个主要瓶颈。目前ASD仅适用于通过Xbox PC应用或微软商店下载的游戏，且需要用户加入Xbox Insider计划并安装Xbox Insider Hub应用来启用该预览功能。微软与AMD密切合作开发了此技术，因此现阶段仅支持RDNA 3及以上架构的AMD GPU，但微软强调未来ASD将作为Windows 11上所有GPU的通用解决方案。

对于NVIDIA用户，其NVIDIA应用中的“自动着色器编译”（Auto Shader Compilation）功能可实现相同效果，并提供更精细的着色器缓存大小控制。英特尔用户则可使用Intel Graphics应用中的“预编译着色器分发”（Precompiled Shader Distribution）功能。英特尔暗示现阶段使用自有的云端数据库，预计今年晚些时候会采用ASD标准。此外，原定支持Xbox ROG Ally掌机的34款以上游戏将立即兼容ASD。这项技术的普及意味着PC游戏启动体验正逐步向主机端靠拢——后者因硬件配置固定，长期采用预编译着色器方案；Valve也曾为Steam Deck的Linux系统开发了类似技术，但未移植至Windows。ASD的推广将显著改善PC玩家的日常游戏体验，尤其对于高频更新和动辄数十款游戏的玩家而言，每次驱动升级后反复等待着色器编译的时代有望终结。

items.title: Jensen Huang slams 'stupid' analogy comparing GPUs to nuclear weapons — Nvidia CEO says government should allow selling GPUs to 'adversarial countries'
items.description: The Nvidia chief said that you cannot compare AI GPUs to nuclear weapons.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 8 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang served as a guest speaker at Stanford’s CS 153 Frontier Systems course and discussed the hardware that powers AI systems today. One of the topics covered in the YouTube session is his stance on granting “adversarial countries” access to Nvidia chips. It’s widely known that the Nvidia chief is against export controls on AI chips, saying that it was a failure and has completely backfired.Other industry leaders do not take a similar stance, with Anthropic's head, Dario Amodei, comparing selling advanced AI chips to China to selling nuclear weapons to North Korea. Jensen did not take this comparison too kindly, saying it does not make any sense.“What I’m fundamentally against, and it makes no sense, it makes no sense in this moment, is to compare Nvidia GPUs to atomic bombs. There are a billion people with Nvidia GPUs; I advocate Nvidia GPUs to all of you, I advocate Nvidia GPUs to my family, my kids, to people I love — but I don’t advocate atomic bombs to anybody,” the Nvidia CEO said. “So that analogy is stupid. And so, so if you start from there, you can’t finish a thought — if you start from believing that, you can’t finish the rest of the thoughts.”Latest Videos From You may like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says China should not have Blackwell or Rubin AI GPUs Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang ‘nearly lost his composure’ when pressed on selling chips to China — ‘You’re not talking to someone who woke up a loser’ Jensen says Nvidia now has 'zero percent' market share in China — says US export policy 'has already largely backfired' Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowJensen Huang is a firm believer that the world should use the American tech stack and that it would be detrimental to the U.S.’s advantage if it were to block a nation from accessing it. Nvidia has a global advantage in that it’s the largest and most popular manufacturer of AI chips, and its technologies, like the CUDA architecture, drive the progress of most of the world’s AI developers. Keeping this technology widely available to anyone would mean that most of the world's AI—whether developed in the U.S. or built in China — runs on American hardware.Jensen Huang responds to his comments about NVIDIA on the Dwarkesh podcast"The idea that I regard as completely ridiculous is why should American companies go compete in foreign countries if you are going to lose it anyway. If you guys all apply that same philosophy, why wake… pic.twitter.com/0e7GazDM9wMay 15, 2026However, critics point out that this could fuel the nation’s adversaries, enabling them to develop and train advanced artificial intelligence for military purposes using Nvidia chips. Jensen said that the Chinese military will avoid U.S. AI tech, much like how the Pentagon does not use Chinese systems. The company also denied providing technical assistance for DeepSeek to improve its training efficiency on models, which were later used by the People’s Liberation Army (PLA). However, public documents revealed that some Chinese universities with deep ties to China’s military-industrial complex acquired Super Micro servers configured with Nvidia A100 AI GPUs.Unlike nuclear missiles and atomic bombs, AI GPUs aren’t strictly military systems designed for a specific mission. Artificial intelligence is a powerful tool that has applications in science, research, business, and many other industries. However, its flexibility also makes it a dual-use technology, meaning it can be used in civilian and military contexts. It is the latter application that has U.S. policymakers worried, in which the same hardware and AI models can be leveraged by armed forces for operational use, such as intelligence and threat analysis, autonomous systems, simulations, and more. This could erode the U.S.’s technological and military edge and give its adversaries a strategic advantage.Both sides of the argument have valid points — America has the advantage as the key provider of AI technologies worldwide, and it makes sense to keep it that way. However, it also does not want its rivals to have access to advanced technologies that could accelerate their capabilities and narrow the United States’ lead in defense technologies. Unfortunately, we can only tell which approach proves to be more effective years, if not decades, from today.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 黄仁勋抨击将GPU比作核武器的“愚蠢”类比——英伟达CEO表示政府应允许向“敌对国家”销售GPU
items.descriptionZh:
Nvidia CEO黄仁勋近日作为斯坦福大学CS 153前沿系统课程的特邀嘉宾，深入探讨了驱动当前AI系统的硬件技术，并再次就美国对华芯片出口管制发表强硬立场。黄仁勋明确反对将AI芯片与核武器类比，称这种比较“愚蠢且毫无意义”。他反驳了Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei将向中国出售先进AI芯片比作向朝鲜出售核武器的观点，指出全球有十亿人使用Nvidia GPU，他会向家人、孩子和所爱之人推荐这些产品，但绝不会向任何人推荐原子弹。他强调，从错误的类比出发无法得出正确结论。

黄仁勋坚持认为，全球应该采用美国技术栈，封锁其他国家的访问将对美国自身优势造成损害。Nvidia凭借CUDA架构等核心技术，已成为全球最大、最流行的AI芯片制造商，驱动着全球大多数AI开发者的工作。保持技术广泛可用意味着无论在美国还是中国开发的AI，都将基于美国硬件运行。然而，批评者指出，这可能会助长对手国家利用Nvidia芯片训练先进AI用于军事目的。黄仁勋回应称，中国军方会像五角大楼不使用中国系统一样避开美国AI技术。尽管Nvidia否认向DeepSeek提供技术援助以提升其训练效率（该模型后被解放军使用），但公开文件显示，一些与中国军工复合体有密切联系的中国大学确实采购了配置Nvidia A100 AI GPU的Supermicro服务器。

与核导弹和原子弹不同，AI GPU并非专为特定军事任务设计的系统。AI作为一种强大的工具，在科学、研究、商业等领域有广泛应用，但其灵活性使其成为“双重用途技术”，可能被民用和军事领域同时利用。美国政策制定者担忧，同一硬件和AI模型可被武装部队用于情报分析、威胁评估、自主系统、模拟等军事行动，这可能削弱美国的技术和军事优势，为对手提供战略筹码。

双方论点各有依据：美国作为全球AI技术的核心供应商，维持这一地位具有战略意义；但另一方面，美国不希望竞争对手获得能加速其能力、缩小国防技术差距的先进技术。哪一种策略更有效，恐怕需要数十年才能验证。黄仁勋在另一场合还表示，美国出口政策“已经基本适得其反”，导致Nvidia在中国市场份额降至“接近零”。这场围绕AI芯片出口管制的辩论，折射出技术全球化与国家安全之间的深层矛盾。

items.title: Maker packs an opinionated, googly-eyed AI chatbot into a mobile suitcase, powered by an Nvidia Jetson — entirely local machine entity runs Gemma 4 E4B and can respond in 200ms
items.description: Sparky is an opinionated googly-eyed AI in a suitcase powered by an Nvidia Jetson Orin NS Super 16GB.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter If you would like a fast, capable, and opinionated robot companion you can chat with anywhere, even without cellular or Wi-Fi connectivity, Redditor CreativelyBankrupt has something to show you. The LLM enthusiast has designed “a fully offline suitcase robot [based-] around a Jetson Orin NS Super 16GB” called Sparky. It runs Gemma 4 E4B locally, includes 30+ sensors for context awareness, and “he has opinions.” You can see what that means in the video embedded below and witness Sparky’s googly eyes in and outside the suitcase.Built a fully offline suitcase robot around a Jetson Orin NX SUPER 16GB. Gemma 4 E4B, ~200ms cached TTFT, 30+ sensors, no WiFi/BT/cellular. He has opinions. from r/LocalLLaMASo, Sparky isn’t just portable; the processing power and sizable array of sensors make him quite a force to be reckoned with when out and about – even off-grid. However, after listening to the conversations in the video, I’d be tempted to leave Sparky at home and take out Marvin the Paranoid Android instead.In the r/LocalLLaMA subreddit, CreativelyBankrupt outlines the ‘recipe’ for this characterful digital companion. “Sparky runs entirely on the Jetson. Gemma 4 E4B at Q4_K_M via llama.cpp with q8_0 KV cache and flash attention. 12K context [conversation memory], native system role,” explains CreativelyBankrupt. Additionally, the wide array of over 30 sensors gives Sparky a window into the real world wherever you care to take him.Latest Videos From You may like Flipper Zero pen-testing tool gets an AI-powered companion app Tech tinkerer gets Gemini to help him 'vibe code' an x86 motherboard design Veteran Windows dev shows off AI running on 47-year-old PDP11 with 6 MHz CPU and 64KB of RAM The suitcase-bound robot is a decent performer, too. A roughly 200 ms Time To First Token (TTFT) means Sparky can start formulating responses very fast, and then runs at about 14-15 tokens per second, according to the LLM enthusiast. What’s more, the response is natural (for a robot), using SenseVoiceSmall for speech-to-text and Piper for text-to-speech. Piper is synced with the PixiJS face, and the mouth animation updates at 43 Hz. CreativelyBankrupt notes that “Vision and OCR are native to Gemma 4 now.” You can also configure and interact with Sparky using a button row, a joystick, and an analog encoder knob.CreativelyBankrupt shared the genesis of Sparky on Reddit, partly to seek hints and tips on optimizing Gemma 4 E4B on Orin-class hardware. However, respondents are mostly in awe of this “cool… adorable… cute… kitschy…” project. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Mark Tyson
items.titleZh: 创客将一个有主见、大眼睛的AI聊天机器人装入移动手提箱，由Nvidia Jetson驱动——完全本地机器实体运行Gemma 4 E4B，可在200毫秒内响应
items.descriptionZh: 一位名叫CreativelyBankrupt的Reddit用户（也是LLM爱好者）打造了一款名为“Sparky”的完全离线行李箱机器人，它基于英伟达Jetson Orin NX Super 16GB边缘计算模组，运行Gemma 4 E4B大语言模型，并配备了超过30个传感器，无需Wi-Fi、蓝牙或蜂窝网络即可随时随地对话。Sparky的核心技术在于其完全本地化的AI推理能力：使用llama.cpp框架，将Gemma 4 E4B模型以Q4_K_M量化精度运行，配合q8_0 KV缓存和Flash Attention机制，原生支持12K token的对话记忆，并内置系统角色设定。这使得Sparky能够独立完成语音识别、视觉理解与光学字符识别（OCR），其中语音识别采用SenseVoiceSmall模型，语音合成使用Piper，并与基于PixiJS的动画面部同步，嘴部动画刷新率高达43Hz。在性能方面，Sparky的首令牌时间（TTFT）仅约200毫秒，响应生成速度达到14-15 tokens/秒，远胜于许多需要云端延迟的同类设备。其创新点在于将大模型推理、多模态感知（视觉、语音、30+传感器情境感知）以及快速交互能力全部压缩进一个便携行李箱中，且完全脱离网络依赖。用户还可以通过按钮、摇杆和模拟编码旋钮进行配置和交互。与类似项目（如Marvin Paranoid Android）相比，Sparky的独特优势在于高效本地化运行与丰富的离线感知融合。该技术影响广泛：它展示了边缘AI芯片（Jetson Orin系列）在运行最新大模型（Gemma 4）时的潜力，为无网络环境下的智能助手、工业巡检、野外探险、紧急通信等场景提供了高性能、低延迟的解决方案。同时，其开源式的搭建方法（依托llama.cpp与量化技术）也促进了低成本、高自主性AI机器人应用的普及。

history.title: Russia’s Mikron is selling framed test wafers with up to 120,000 processors as souvenirs — 12 designs, priced around $170 each, sold alongside $2 vials of cleanroom air
history.description: Russia’s Mikron is earning a bit of cash on the side by selling picture-framed test wafers as souvenirs with 12 designs to choose from, priced around $170.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Russia’s Mikron is earning a bit of cash on the side by selling picture-framed test wafers as souvenirs. There are 12 designs in the product gallery, all priced at 12,500 rubles (~$170), with various frame backgrounds and commemorative texts. Mikron says supplies of these exclusive silicon wafer artworks are limited. (Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingSpotted by Dmitrii Kuznetsov @torgeek on X, some of these wafers will feature the AMUR MIK32 RISC-V chip, produced in Russia since 2022. The computer and data science enthusiast also notes that Mikron is busy “preparing to release a new RISC-V — MIK32-2.” (machine translation)The souvenir product pages also reveal that the framed 200mm wafers may also come packing between 30 to 120,000 chips, depending on the product. For example, one of the wafer designs may be full of transport card chips as used by the Moscow Metro.Latest Videos From You may like 11-month old Russian outfit claims it has developed 16-core and 32-core chips, flaunts Cyrillic-badged chips 'Silicon' is a new five-pound art book charting the semiconductor revolution with full-page die shots and commentary Nexperia China claims to be making chips using 12-inch wafers Buyers visiting the Mikorn souvenir shop pages will choose their framed wafer by selecting the frame color and/or theme. For example, there are products with simple black or white frames. But there are other designs with lace, paisley, or even an outer space theme. What kind of wafer you get with your choice of frame, appears to be pot luck.We used machine translation on the new 2026 edition wafer souvenirs, to see what the information panel to the lower left of the frame said. The top row of the table reads: 2026 | Made in Russia | Techno exclusive | Russian chips | 200mm. Moving to the second row: Silicon wafer with integrated circuits | The satellite plate is used in the technological process for quality control at all stages of microcircuit production.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)(Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)(Image credit: Mikron souvenirs)If you manage to buy one of these Russian semiconductor industry souvenirs, you will need a space on your wall for a 270 x 270 x 15mm (10.6 x 10.6 x 0.6-inches) frame, which weighs 365g (~13 ounces). I think they look smart, but it would be even better if you had a piece of tech that actually used one of the chips from the wafers for your wall presentation.If you have followed the link to Mikron’s souvenir shop and had a browse around, like us, there are a few other interesting trinkets to see. A much cheaper souvenir flagged as ‘new’ is the small tube of air from the firm’s NWP clean room in Zelenograd, priced at around $2. Elsewhere there are gift shop trinkets of the style you might find anywhere, like dolls, mugs, magnets, and puzzles.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: 俄罗斯Mikron公司出售装裱测试晶圆作为纪念品——内含多达12万个处理器，12种设计，每片约170美元，同时销售2美元一瓶的洁净室空气
history.descriptionZh:
俄罗斯芯片制造商Mikron正通过销售嵌有测试晶圆的装饰画框来获取额外收入。这些晶圆纪念品共有12种设计，售价统一为12,500卢布（约合170美元），可根据客户需求选择不同的画框背景和纪念文字。Mikron表示，这些独家硅晶圆艺术品的供应量有限。

这些晶圆中的一部分搭载了AMUR MIK32 RISC-V芯片——该芯片自2022年起在俄罗斯量产。据计算机和数据科学爱好者Dmitrii Kuznetsov在X平台上爆料，Mikron正在“准备发布新款RISC-V芯片——MIK32-2”（机器翻译）。此外，纪念品页面显示，这些镶嵌在画框中的200毫米晶圆可能包含30至12万颗芯片，具体数量取决于产品类型。例如，其中一款晶圆设计上密集排列着莫斯科地铁使用的交通卡芯片。

从技术原理角度看，这些晶圆本质上是半导体制造过程中的测试晶圆（test wafers），通常用于质量控制和工艺验证。它们是在晶圆厂中经过完整光刻、刻蚀、沉积等步骤生产的，但最终并不封装成独立芯片，而是作为检查光刻对准精度、膜厚均匀性、缺陷密度等参数的标准片。Mikron将其作为纪念品出售，既展现了其制造工艺，又实现了废物利用。其中搭载的RISC-V芯片属于开源指令集架构，具有模块化、低功耗、可定制等优势，AMUR MIK32是俄罗斯首款基于RISC-V的微控制器，用于物联网和嵌入式场景。而MIK32-2可能是在性能、功耗或集成度上进一步优化的迭代版本。

在性能参数方面，晶圆直径为200毫米（约8英寸），属于成熟制程常用的规格（相比当前主流的300毫米晶圆，200毫米产线更适合小批量、多品种的模拟/混合信号芯片）。单张晶圆上芯片数量从30到12万颗不等，差异极大，说明这些晶圆可能来自不同设计或不同工艺节点（例如大尺寸的微控制器芯片数量少，而小尺寸的交通卡芯片数量多）。画框成品尺寸为270×270×15毫米（约10.6×10.6×0.6英寸），重量365克（约13盎司），适合挂墙展示。

除晶圆画框外，Mikron商店还出售更具噱头的纪念品：一管来自其Zelenograd洁净室的“空气”，售价约2美元，以及常规的玩偶、马克杯、磁贴、拼图等。值得注意的是，晶圆画框左下角的信息标签（据机器翻译）显示：“2026 | 俄罗斯制造 | 技术独家 | 俄罗斯芯片 | 200毫米”，第二行写着“带集成电路的硅晶圆 | 该卫星板用于微电路生产所有阶段的质量控制工艺”。这暗示这些晶圆可能来自2026年（或许是未来纪念版），并且强调了其作为质量监控“卫星片”（monitor wafer）的工业用途。

技术影响和应用场景方面：Mikron此举不仅是一种商业尝试，更具有技术品牌传播意义。通过将高价值的半导体制造过程转化为艺术藏品，它向公众展示了俄罗斯在芯片制造领域的能力——尽管受西方制裁限制，但依然具备200毫米晶圆量产能力（主要用于成熟制程，如90nm及以上节点）。对于收藏者和科技爱好者来说，这些晶圆提供了直观了解芯片微观结构的窗口。同时，这也反映了俄罗斯半导体产业在寻求多元化收入，以及通过RISC-V架构实现技术自主的战略方向。未来，若MIK32-2芯片成功量产，可能进一步推动俄罗斯物联网、智能卡、工业控制等领域摆脱对国外架构的依赖。而晶圆纪念品的销售，也能在一定程度上向全球展示其技术成果，甚至作为外交或文化输出的载体。

history.title: Forza Horizon 6 boots up in just 4 seconds instead of 90 with new Advanced Shader Delivery tech and AMD GPUs — Microsoft claims 95% reduction in gaming load times
history.description: Microsoft is bringing Advanced Shader Delivery to Windows 11 PCs at large, after introducing the tech on Xbox ROG Ally handhelds last year. The company says you can expect up to 95% faster load times in Forza Horizon 6, for instance, going from 90 seconds to just 4 seconds on initial launch thanks to precompiled shaders.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft announced Advanced Shader Delivery (ASD) for Xbox ROG Ally devices last year, bringing precompiled shaders to the handheld to improve load times. Since then, ASD has been included in the DirectX SDK, with both Intel and Nvidia already releasing their own versions of the tech. Today, AMD joins them as Microsoft expands ASD beyond handhelds to RDNA 3, RDNA 3.5, and RDNA 4 GPUs.Forza Horizon 6 is the latest game to feature ASD on Windows 11 PCs, but you need the Microsoft Store/Xbox PC app version to take advantage of it. Using an RX 7600 GPU and a Ryzen 7 5800 CPU, Advanced Shader Delivery helped the game boot up 95% faster, taking only four seconds to load on first launch. Without ASD enabled, Forza Horizon 6 took nearly a minute and a half to load otherwise.This is because every time you install a new game or go through an update, the game needs a fresh shader cache, which can take a few minutes to rebuild. Even if you don't touch the game, but your GPU drivers are updated, you need to recompile shaders for all of your games when you open them for the first time. This process is necessary because shader compilation is performed on-device and must account for hardware variability.Latest Videos From (Image credit: Microsoft)Microsoft's solution is to decouple shaders from drivers entirely and place them in a Precompiled Shader Database (PSDB) that lives in the cloud. Every time you download a game from the Microsoft Store or the Xbox PC app, ASD detects your specific configuration (game, GPU, driver) and downloads the precompiled shaders in advance. So, when you open the game, the shaders are already compiled, and you don't have to wait.Consoles have done this forever, which makes sense considering they don't have to worry about different hardware configs. Even Valve has a version of precompiled shaders for Linux that it developed for the Steam Deck, but never ported to Windows. Since SSDs have made long load times a thing of the past, this has been one of the last remaining hurdles to instantaneous game launches. "PC Gaming" preview in the Xbox Insider Hub (Image credit: Future)Advanced Shader Delivery is available only on RDNA 3 and later GPUs and only on games downloaded via the Xbox PC app or the Microsoft Store. We don't know when other Windows marketplaces will adopt it, but at least 34 other games should support ASD right away, since they were part of the original announcement for the Xbox ROG Ally handhelds. Moreover, you need to be enrolled in the Xbox Insiders program to get the Xbox Insiders Hub app, which is required to enable Advanced Shader Delivery, since Microsoft is classifying this as a preview. The company worked closely with AMD to develop it, which is why it's limited to RDNA 3+ hardware for now, because otherwise, it's supposed to be a universal solution for all GPUs on Windows 11 going forward.Regardless, if you have an Nvidia GPU, the Nvidia app should have a feature called "Auto Shader Compilation" that does the same thing. It gives you more granular control over the size of the shader cache, too. If you're rocking an Intel GPU, then check out "Precompiled Shader Distribution" in the Intel Graphics app instead. Intel has implied that it's using its own cloud database for now, with the ASD standard being adopted later this year. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 借助新高级着色器传输技术和AMD GPU，《极限竞速：地平线6》启动仅需4秒而非90秒——微软声称游戏加载时间减少95%
history.descriptionZh:
微软宣布将其高级着色器交付（Advanced Shader Delivery，ASD）技术扩展至AMD RDNA 3、RDNA 3.5和RDNA 4架构的GPU，结束了此前仅限Xbox ROG Ally掌机以及英特尔和NVIDIA单独实现该功能的局面。ASD的核心意义在于解决PC游戏启动时因着色器编译导致的漫长等待——每次安装新游戏、更新游戏或升级显卡驱动后，系统都需要在本地重新编译着色器缓存，这一过程可能耗时数分钟。微软的方案是将着色器与驱动彻底解耦，将其预编译后存储于云端“预编译着色器数据库”（PSDB）。当用户从微软商店或Xbox PC应用下载游戏时，ASD会自动识别用户的游戏、GPU和驱动配置，提前下载匹配的预编译着色器，从而在首次启动时无需等待编译，实现即开即玩。

在性能测试中，使用RX 7600显卡和Ryzen 7 5800 CPU的PC运行《极限竞速：地平线6》时，开启ASD后游戏首次启动仅需4秒，相比未开启时近90秒的加载时间，提速高达95%。这一提升直接消除了SSD普及后游戏启动的最后一个主要瓶颈。目前ASD仅适用于通过Xbox PC应用或微软商店下载的游戏，且需要用户加入Xbox Insider计划并安装Xbox Insider Hub应用来启用该预览功能。微软与AMD密切合作开发了此技术，因此现阶段仅支持RDNA 3及以上架构的AMD GPU，但微软强调未来ASD将作为Windows 11上所有GPU的通用解决方案。

对于NVIDIA用户，其NVIDIA应用中的“自动着色器编译”（Auto Shader Compilation）功能可实现相同效果，并提供更精细的着色器缓存大小控制。英特尔用户则可使用Intel Graphics应用中的“预编译着色器分发”（Precompiled Shader Distribution）功能。英特尔暗示现阶段使用自有的云端数据库，预计今年晚些时候会采用ASD标准。此外，原定支持Xbox ROG Ally掌机的34款以上游戏将立即兼容ASD。这项技术的普及意味着PC游戏启动体验正逐步向主机端靠拢——后者因硬件配置固定，长期采用预编译着色器方案；Valve也曾为Steam Deck的Linux系统开发了类似技术，但未移植至Windows。ASD的推广将显著改善PC玩家的日常游戏体验，尤其对于高频更新和动辄数十款游戏的玩家而言，每次驱动升级后反复等待着色器编译的时代有望终结。

history.title: Jensen Huang slams 'stupid' analogy comparing GPUs to nuclear weapons — Nvidia CEO says government should allow selling GPUs to 'adversarial countries'
history.description: The Nvidia chief said that you cannot compare AI GPUs to nuclear weapons.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 8 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang served as a guest speaker at Stanford’s CS 153 Frontier Systems course and discussed the hardware that powers AI systems today. One of the topics covered in the YouTube session is his stance on granting “adversarial countries” access to Nvidia chips. It’s widely known that the Nvidia chief is against export controls on AI chips, saying that it was a failure and has completely backfired.Other industry leaders do not take a similar stance, with Anthropic's head, Dario Amodei, comparing selling advanced AI chips to China to selling nuclear weapons to North Korea. Jensen did not take this comparison too kindly, saying it does not make any sense.“What I’m fundamentally against, and it makes no sense, it makes no sense in this moment, is to compare Nvidia GPUs to atomic bombs. There are a billion people with Nvidia GPUs; I advocate Nvidia GPUs to all of you, I advocate Nvidia GPUs to my family, my kids, to people I love — but I don’t advocate atomic bombs to anybody,” the Nvidia CEO said. “So that analogy is stupid. And so, so if you start from there, you can’t finish a thought — if you start from believing that, you can’t finish the rest of the thoughts.”Latest Videos From You may like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says China should not have Blackwell or Rubin AI GPUs Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang ‘nearly lost his composure’ when pressed on selling chips to China — ‘You’re not talking to someone who woke up a loser’ Jensen says Nvidia now has 'zero percent' market share in China — says US export policy 'has already largely backfired' Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowJensen Huang is a firm believer that the world should use the American tech stack and that it would be detrimental to the U.S.’s advantage if it were to block a nation from accessing it. Nvidia has a global advantage in that it’s the largest and most popular manufacturer of AI chips, and its technologies, like the CUDA architecture, drive the progress of most of the world’s AI developers. Keeping this technology widely available to anyone would mean that most of the world's AI—whether developed in the U.S. or built in China — runs on American hardware.Jensen Huang responds to his comments about NVIDIA on the Dwarkesh podcast"The idea that I regard as completely ridiculous is why should American companies go compete in foreign countries if you are going to lose it anyway. If you guys all apply that same philosophy, why wake… pic.twitter.com/0e7GazDM9wMay 15, 2026However, critics point out that this could fuel the nation’s adversaries, enabling them to develop and train advanced artificial intelligence for military purposes using Nvidia chips. Jensen said that the Chinese military will avoid U.S. AI tech, much like how the Pentagon does not use Chinese systems. The company also denied providing technical assistance for DeepSeek to improve its training efficiency on models, which were later used by the People’s Liberation Army (PLA). However, public documents revealed that some Chinese universities with deep ties to China’s military-industrial complex acquired Super Micro servers configured with Nvidia A100 AI GPUs.Unlike nuclear missiles and atomic bombs, AI GPUs aren’t strictly military systems designed for a specific mission. Artificial intelligence is a powerful tool that has applications in science, research, business, and many other industries. However, its flexibility also makes it a dual-use technology, meaning it can be used in civilian and military contexts. It is the latter application that has U.S. policymakers worried, in which the same hardware and AI models can be leveraged by armed forces for operational use, such as intelligence and threat analysis, autonomous systems, simulations, and more. This could erode the U.S.’s technological and military edge and give its adversaries a strategic advantage.Both sides of the argument have valid points — America has the advantage as the key provider of AI technologies worldwide, and it makes sense to keep it that way. However, it also does not want its rivals to have access to advanced technologies that could accelerate their capabilities and narrow the United States’ lead in defense technologies. Unfortunately, we can only tell which approach proves to be more effective years, if not decades, from today.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 黄仁勋抨击将GPU比作核武器的“愚蠢”类比——英伟达CEO表示政府应允许向“敌对国家”销售GPU
history.descriptionZh:
Nvidia CEO黄仁勋近日作为斯坦福大学CS 153前沿系统课程的特邀嘉宾，深入探讨了驱动当前AI系统的硬件技术，并再次就美国对华芯片出口管制发表强硬立场。黄仁勋明确反对将AI芯片与核武器类比，称这种比较“愚蠢且毫无意义”。他反驳了Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei将向中国出售先进AI芯片比作向朝鲜出售核武器的观点，指出全球有十亿人使用Nvidia GPU，他会向家人、孩子和所爱之人推荐这些产品，但绝不会向任何人推荐原子弹。他强调，从错误的类比出发无法得出正确结论。

黄仁勋坚持认为，全球应该采用美国技术栈，封锁其他国家的访问将对美国自身优势造成损害。Nvidia凭借CUDA架构等核心技术，已成为全球最大、最流行的AI芯片制造商，驱动着全球大多数AI开发者的工作。保持技术广泛可用意味着无论在美国还是中国开发的AI，都将基于美国硬件运行。然而，批评者指出，这可能会助长对手国家利用Nvidia芯片训练先进AI用于军事目的。黄仁勋回应称，中国军方会像五角大楼不使用中国系统一样避开美国AI技术。尽管Nvidia否认向DeepSeek提供技术援助以提升其训练效率（该模型后被解放军使用），但公开文件显示，一些与中国军工复合体有密切联系的中国大学确实采购了配置Nvidia A100 AI GPU的Supermicro服务器。

与核导弹和原子弹不同，AI GPU并非专为特定军事任务设计的系统。AI作为一种强大的工具，在科学、研究、商业等领域有广泛应用，但其灵活性使其成为“双重用途技术”，可能被民用和军事领域同时利用。美国政策制定者担忧，同一硬件和AI模型可被武装部队用于情报分析、威胁评估、自主系统、模拟等军事行动，这可能削弱美国的技术和军事优势，为对手提供战略筹码。

双方论点各有依据：美国作为全球AI技术的核心供应商，维持这一地位具有战略意义；但另一方面，美国不希望竞争对手获得能加速其能力、缩小国防技术差距的先进技术。哪一种策略更有效，恐怕需要数十年才能验证。黄仁勋在另一场合还表示，美国出口政策“已经基本适得其反”，导致Nvidia在中国市场份额降至“接近零”。这场围绕AI芯片出口管制的辩论，折射出技术全球化与国家安全之间的深层矛盾。

history.title: Maker packs an opinionated, googly-eyed AI chatbot into a mobile suitcase, powered by an Nvidia Jetson — entirely local machine entity runs Gemma 4 E4B and can respond in 200ms
history.description: Sparky is an opinionated googly-eyed AI in a suitcase powered by an Nvidia Jetson Orin NS Super 16GB.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter If you would like a fast, capable, and opinionated robot companion you can chat with anywhere, even without cellular or Wi-Fi connectivity, Redditor CreativelyBankrupt has something to show you. The LLM enthusiast has designed “a fully offline suitcase robot [based-] around a Jetson Orin NS Super 16GB” called Sparky. It runs Gemma 4 E4B locally, includes 30+ sensors for context awareness, and “he has opinions.” You can see what that means in the video embedded below and witness Sparky’s googly eyes in and outside the suitcase.Built a fully offline suitcase robot around a Jetson Orin NX SUPER 16GB. Gemma 4 E4B, ~200ms cached TTFT, 30+ sensors, no WiFi/BT/cellular. He has opinions. from r/LocalLLaMASo, Sparky isn’t just portable; the processing power and sizable array of sensors make him quite a force to be reckoned with when out and about – even off-grid. However, after listening to the conversations in the video, I’d be tempted to leave Sparky at home and take out Marvin the Paranoid Android instead.In the r/LocalLLaMA subreddit, CreativelyBankrupt outlines the ‘recipe’ for this characterful digital companion. “Sparky runs entirely on the Jetson. Gemma 4 E4B at Q4_K_M via llama.cpp with q8_0 KV cache and flash attention. 12K context [conversation memory], native system role,” explains CreativelyBankrupt. Additionally, the wide array of over 30 sensors gives Sparky a window into the real world wherever you care to take him.Latest Videos From You may like Flipper Zero pen-testing tool gets an AI-powered companion app Tech tinkerer gets Gemini to help him 'vibe code' an x86 motherboard design Veteran Windows dev shows off AI running on 47-year-old PDP11 with 6 MHz CPU and 64KB of RAM The suitcase-bound robot is a decent performer, too. A roughly 200 ms Time To First Token (TTFT) means Sparky can start formulating responses very fast, and then runs at about 14-15 tokens per second, according to the LLM enthusiast. What’s more, the response is natural (for a robot), using SenseVoiceSmall for speech-to-text and Piper for text-to-speech. Piper is synced with the PixiJS face, and the mouth animation updates at 43 Hz. CreativelyBankrupt notes that “Vision and OCR are native to Gemma 4 now.” You can also configure and interact with Sparky using a button row, a joystick, and an analog encoder knob.CreativelyBankrupt shared the genesis of Sparky on Reddit, partly to seek hints and tips on optimizing Gemma 4 E4B on Orin-class hardware. However, respondents are mostly in awe of this “cool… adorable… cute… kitschy…” project. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: 创客将一个有主见、大眼睛的AI聊天机器人装入移动手提箱，由Nvidia Jetson驱动——完全本地机器实体运行Gemma 4 E4B，可在200毫秒内响应
history.descriptionZh: 一位名叫CreativelyBankrupt的Reddit用户（也是LLM爱好者）打造了一款名为“Sparky”的完全离线行李箱机器人，它基于英伟达Jetson Orin NX Super 16GB边缘计算模组，运行Gemma 4 E4B大语言模型，并配备了超过30个传感器，无需Wi-Fi、蓝牙或蜂窝网络即可随时随地对话。Sparky的核心技术在于其完全本地化的AI推理能力：使用llama.cpp框架，将Gemma 4 E4B模型以Q4_K_M量化精度运行，配合q8_0 KV缓存和Flash Attention机制，原生支持12K token的对话记忆，并内置系统角色设定。这使得Sparky能够独立完成语音识别、视觉理解与光学字符识别（OCR），其中语音识别采用SenseVoiceSmall模型，语音合成使用Piper，并与基于PixiJS的动画面部同步，嘴部动画刷新率高达43Hz。在性能方面，Sparky的首令牌时间（TTFT）仅约200毫秒，响应生成速度达到14-15 tokens/秒，远胜于许多需要云端延迟的同类设备。其创新点在于将大模型推理、多模态感知（视觉、语音、30+传感器情境感知）以及快速交互能力全部压缩进一个便携行李箱中，且完全脱离网络依赖。用户还可以通过按钮、摇杆和模拟编码旋钮进行配置和交互。与类似项目（如Marvin Paranoid Android）相比，Sparky的独特优势在于高效本地化运行与丰富的离线感知融合。该技术影响广泛：它展示了边缘AI芯片（Jetson Orin系列）在运行最新大模型（Gemma 4）时的潜力，为无网络环境下的智能助手、工业巡检、野外探险、紧急通信等场景提供了高性能、低延迟的解决方案。同时，其开源式的搭建方法（依托llama.cpp与量化技术）也促进了低成本、高自主性AI机器人应用的普及。
